business.headline: Have you recieved this 'total TDS' message? Here's what it meansThe Income Tax Department has started sending 'Total TDS' SMS alerts to taxpayers across the country. Why are you receiving this message, and what does it mean for your ITR? Have a look.

Inflation jumps from 2.74% to 4.38% in 6 months: Why did it rise so sharply?Food inflation climbed to 5.32% from 4.78% a month earlier. Since food carries the highest weight in the inflation basket, higher food prices had the biggest impact on overall inflation.

Why South Korea's KOSPI, world's best-performing stock market, is suddenly crashingSouth Korea's KOSPI has slipped into a bear market after a record AI-driven surge led by Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. The reversal has exposed heavy retail leverage, foreign outflows and regulator concerns over single-stock products.

EPFO simplifies PF transfers: What the new rule means for job switchersThe change has been introduced after EPFO shifted to its Centralised IT Enabled Services (CITES) platform, which is designed to bring member records and services onto a single centralised system.

SBI Funds Management IPO GMP hints at 16% listing gains, but should you subscribe?SBI Funds Management is India's largest asset management company (AMC) and manages the SBI Mutual Fund. It is a joint venture between State Bank of India and French asset manager Amundi.

Trump wants a 20% Hormuz toll. What it could mean for India's fuel billIndia is in a better position to weather a disruption than it was a few months ago, but any prolonged instability around Hormuz could still push up oil prices, freight costs and inflation.

EPFO's plan to raise wage ceiling to Rs 25,000 put on hold. Here's whyAt present, the statutory wage ceiling for mandatory contributions under the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) and Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) is Rs 15,000 a month. The limit has remained unchanged since 2014.
Sensex, Nifty open lower as oil prices jump on fresh US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 314.58 points to 77,301.82, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 85.45 points to 24,125.55 as of 9:35 am.

Nifty at 26,500? Goldman Sachs says these stocks, sectors could lead the rallyGoldman Sachs expects the benchmark index to reach 26,500, implying an upside of around 9.5% from Monday's closing level of 24,211. But more importantly for investors, it has identified the sectors and stocks it believes are best placed to benefit if the market recovery gathers pace.

SBI Funds Management IPO opens today: Should you subscribe? Check GMP, detailsThe SBI Funds Management IPO opens for subscription on July 14, 2026, and closes on July 16, 2026. The IPO is entirely an Offer for Sale (OFS), which means the company will not receive any money from the issue. Instead, the existing shareholders are selling part of their stake.

Why HDFC Bank reduced its workforce by over 3,300 despite growing businessThe annual report shows that HDFC Bank's total employee strength stood at 2,11,178 as of March 31, 2026, down by 3,343 employees from the previous year

Russia, Iran, North Korea moved $104 billion in crypto to bypass sanctions: ReportChainalysis says Russia, Iran and North Korea used cryptocurrencies to keep funds moving under sanctions in 2025. The report points to a fast-growing digital route for sanctions evasion, theft and restricted transactions.

BSNL's core services revenue rises 10% in Q1FY27 as govt pushes for its turnaroundBSNL's core services revenue rose to Rs 4,418 crore in Q1FY27 from 4,017 crore in the corresponding quarter last year, driven largely by growth in its Enterprise Business and Consumer Mobility segments.

India-US trade deal framework ready as talks progress amid tariff uncertaintyIn February, the two sides agreed to 18% tariffs on Indian goods in exchange for New Delhi lowering trade barriers and buying more American goods. But a final deal has been clouded by the U.S. Supreme Court ruling invalidating President Donald Trump's sweeping global tariffs.

About 2 crore ITRs filed so far. Should you wait for a deadline extension?With around two crore returns already filed and the filing process running smoothly this year, taxpayers may be better off completing their returns well before the deadline.

From Rs 5,000 late fee to 200% penalty: Income tax mistakes that could cost you bigA small mistake in your income tax compliance can turn into a big financial setback. From late filing fees to steep penalties for under-reporting income, the Income Tax Act prescribes strict consequences for different types of defaults. Are you aware of the most common ones?

June retail inflation climbs to 4.38%, breaches RBI's 4% target after 17 monthsThe latest reading marks the first time in 17 months that headline inflation has crossed the RBI's 4% target. Retail inflation had eased to 2.74% in January before gradually rising to 3.21% in February, 3.40% in March, 3.48% in April and 3.93% in May.

Piyush Goyal dismisses report claiming India is holding back on US trade dealThe rebuttal comes at a sensitive stage in India-US trade negotiations, with both sides racing to bridge differences on tariffs and market access ahead of a proposed bilateral trade agreement.

Why SK Hynix shares crashed over 15% days after their Nasdaq debutJust days after making a strong start in the US market, the South Korean chipmaker's shares plunged more than 15% on Monday, as investors rushed to book profits.

Delhi's property boom is creating an unusual courtroom problemWhat do soaring home prices have to do with courtrooms? According to the Delhi High Court, more than you might think. Here's the unusual problem now under scrutiny.

entertainment.headline: Supreme Court dismisses plea against renovation of Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat The Supreme Court dismissed a plea challenging the CRZ clearance for renovation work at Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat. The order leaves the NGT ruling untouched and keeps approval for the proposed two additional floors in place.

CM Vijay's childlike excitement over miniature EV car goes viral. Watch video At a Chennai Secretariat meeting, Chief Minister Joseph Vijay was seen fiddling with a miniature VinFast VF 8 electric SUV presented by Pham Sanh Chau. The candid exchange drew amused reactions online as fans said it revealed his playful side amid work and film commitments.

Ashutosh Gowariker on helming Temple Raiders: There was anger and compassion Ashutosh Gowariker said Temple Raiders was driven by anger, compassion and alarm over idol theft. He said the series aims to raise awareness and acknowledge efforts to recover India's stolen heritage.

India's Got Latent Season 2 more contained, says Kiku Sharda; out-of-line jokes dropped Actor-comedian Kiku Sharda opened up about the changes in India's Got Latent Season 2, saying comedian Samay Raina is now more careful with his humour. He also shared that some hilarious but 'out of line' moments were removed before the episode was released.

After Zeenat Aman, Abhay Deol backs Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike Actor Abhay Deol shared a photograph from Sonam Wangchuk's protest site as the activist's indefinite hunger strike entered its 17th day.

What Aamir couldn't do: Internet rallies behind 3 Idiots' Chatur over Wangchuk video Actor Omi Vaidya voiced support for Sonam Wangchuk as the activist's hunger strike entered its 16th day at Jantar Mantar. His appeal triggered strong social media backing, with users invoking 3 Idiots and raising concern over Wangchuk's health.

Is internet happy about Alia Bhatt joining Tumbbad 2? Check reactions Alia Bhatt has joined Tumbbad 2 alongside Sohum Shah and Nawazuddin Siddiqui. The announcement has split viewers, with some excited and others worried about the sequel's tone.

Chiranjeevi steps in to end Telugu dance choreographers Jani and Sekhar's feud Choreographer Jani Master said there is no personal conflict with Sekhar Master and said Chiranjeevi brought them together on a film set. The clarification comes after videos of heated discussion between both choreographers circulated widely on social media.

Main virgin hoon...: Internet shreds Shilpa Shinde for taunting Shivangi on Lock Upp Actor Shilpa Shinde has come under fire after a viral clip from Lock Upp showed her making a 'virgin' remark while mocking her co-contestant Shivangi Joshi. The video has sparked outrage online, with several users calling out the actor over her comments.

Shivangi Joshi's sister blasts Shilpa Shinde for disrespecting her 'butterfly girl' Actor Shivangi Joshi's sister Sheetal publicly defended her after a Lock Upp: Sach ya Sazaa episode showed Shilpa Shinde asking the unwell actor to clean alone. The exchange sparked online outrage, with viewers backing Shivangi and praising her composure.

Was Fahadh Faasil offered role in Tom Cruise's Digger? Viral fan theory explained Fahadh Faasil's account of stepping away from an Alejandro Gonzalez Inarritu's film has resurfaced after the Digger trailer launch. The timing has triggered speculation that he may have passed on the role now played by Riz Ahmed, though there is no confirmation.

Zeenat Aman's passionate appeal to government over Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike Zeenat Aman backed Sonam Wangchuk as his indefinite hunger strike in Delhi entered its 17th day. She urged the government to open dialogue, saying peaceful protest must be met with peaceful dialogue.

Don't judge my grief by tears you don't see: S Janaki's granddaughter in moving note S Janaki's granddaughter posted a note asking people not to judge her grief by her tears after the singer's death. She said loss is deeply personal, recalled her late sister Varsha, and spoke of the pain of public scrutiny.

I was written off: Govinda announces Roopa, his first film in seven years Bollywood actor Govinda announced his comeback film Roopa, marking his return to the big screen after seven years. The actor reflected on career setbacks and said he hopes the film strikes a chord with young audiences.

Actor Anuj Sachdeva slams Hina, Rubina for remarks on Gaurav Khanna's personal life A discussion on The POV about Gaurav Khanna and Akanksha Chamola's choice not to have children drew sharp attention online. Anuj Sachdev's reaction intensified the row and renewed debate over comments on the couple's private life.

The Vampire Diaries star Paul Wesley marries girlfriend Natalie Kuckenburg Actor Paul Wesley and girlfriend Natalie Kuckenburg have married in a quiet ceremony attended only by a minister and their dog Greg. The couple dated for several years and got engaged in 2025.

Anurag Kashyap watches Mukundan Unni Associates, calls it absolute banger Anurag Kashyap watched Mukundan Unni Associates with director Abhinav Sunder Nayak and praised it warmly. His signed note, Letterboxd review and Vineeth Sreenivasan's response amplified the film's buzz.

Alia Bhatt joins Tumbbad 2 with Sohum Shah and Nawazuddin Siddiqui Alia Bhatt has confirmed she will star in Tumbbad 2 alongside Sohum Shah and Nawazuddin Siddiqui. Her announcement ends weeks of speculation and reflects the original film's lasting pull on audiences.

Don't let Phunsukh Wangdu die, 3 Idiots' Chatur appeals Wangchuk hunger strike Actor Omi Vaidya urged people to pay attention to Sonam Wangchuk's indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. His appeal came as concern deepened over Wangchuk's worsening health and the protest's demands.

general news.headline: Targeting of commercial shipping must cease: Delhi after Hormuz strikes kill IndianThe statement came shortly after the Ministry of External Affairs summoned the Iranian Embassy's Deputy Chief of Mission in New Delhi and lodged a "strong protest" over the missile strike on two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz that left one Indian seafarer dead and several others injured.

Average car owner can't check mileage on their own: Nitin Gadkari amid E20 rowThe minister said car owners cannot accurately measure mileage themselves and should depend on readings taken at authorised dealers. His remarks came amid growing complaints over the government's ethanol blending mandate.

Pay Trump's Hormuz hafta or risk sleeping with the fishesAfter igniting the Middle East conflict and imperilling the Strait of Hormuz, US President Donald Trump said ships will be charged a fee for safe passage through the critical trade route. The proposal is not just brazen but downright fanciful.

The 136-year-old mosque that refused to move from Kolkata airportOver the decades, a mosque standing right within the premises of Kolkata airport has remained a point of contention. In fact, the Bankra mosque predates the airport. Now, the BJP government in Bengal has renewed efforts to relocate the mosque.

Meet the IAS officer leading Maharashtra's toughest food safety crackdownMaharashtra's FDA has launched a statewide drive against milk adulteration under commissioner Tukaram Mundhe. The action has exposed synthetic milk units, disrupted supplies and sparked calls for compliance time.

1st ODI Live Score: Toss at 3 pm, all eyes on Rohit-Kohli returnEngland (ENG) vs India (IND), 1st ODI Live Score: After the disappointment of the T20I series, India will be desperate to find their feet when they take on England in the first ODI at Edgbaston in Birmingham. The returns of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma, and Jasprit Bumrah have bolstered the visitors as they look to bounce back in the 50-over format.

Bhojshala temple dispute: Supreme Court allows Friday namaz near siteThe Supreme Court has declined to pause the Madhya Pradesh High Court's May verdict declaring the Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque complex in Dhar district as a temple.

Monsoon stirring again: New system in Bay of Bengal to revive rainAfter days of dry weather and a slowing monsoon, a fresh weather system is offering hope that widespread rainfall could soon return across India.

What's kharku in Satluj? Why Khalistanis beheaded AIR Patiala station directorThe use of the word "kharku" for Khalistani terrorists in Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj has reignited a debate over the vocabulary used in the film now taken down by ZEE5. What does the word mean? How's it linked to the beheading of ML Manchanda, the station director of AIR Patiala, by Babbar Khalsa?
Why Gadkari is on the road, Puri in blended oilNitin Gadkari is back monitoring highways and Hardeep Singh Puri is defending the ethanol policy. What's behind the reshuffle that's confusing people?

Don't let Phunsukh Wangdu die, 3 Idiots' Chatur appeals Wangchuk hunger strikeActor Omi Vaidya urged people to pay attention to Sonam Wangchuk's indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. His appeal came as concern deepened over Wangchuk's worsening health and the protest's demands.

Why the football world has turned against ArgentinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's rise from Messi's tragic nearly-men to world champions has changed how football fans view them. Their success now attracts suspicion, backlash and scrutiny that often follows title-winning sides.

What is Pickaxe Mountain, Iran's secretive nuclear site in Trump's sights?Trump's warning has renewed focus on Pickaxe Mountain, a heavily fortified underground complex that experts say could lie beyond the reach of US bunker-buster bombs. Satellite imagery has shown continued activity there, keeping alive questions over Iran's nuclear programme despite last year's strikes.

CBSE's 3-language policy got another update. 8 biggest changes since June circularCBSE has issued another clarification on its three-language policy, this time answering practical questions left open after the June 29 circular. From what happens if a student fails the third language to migration rules, teacher arrangements and language combinations, here are the biggest updates explained in simple terms.

Elephant returns 14 years later to kill 2 more members of same Nepali familyFour members of the same Nepal family were killed by a rogue elephant over 14 years, even after the family moved away hoping to escape the animal's deadly path.

Maharashtra's Ladki Bahin scheme faces fresh heat as women allege payments stoppedWith Rakhi approaching, thousands of women say the Rs 1,500 they depended on has suddenly stopped. As the government cites verification, a CAG report and ground reality raise uncomfortable questions.

Sonam Wangchuk on hunger strike: What happens to human body while fastingSonam Wangchuk completes 16 days without food, doctors explain how the human body shifts from burning sugar to fat and why the health risks rise sharply after two weeks.

Have you recieved this 'total TDS' message? Here's what it meansThe Income Tax Department has started sending 'Total TDS' SMS alerts to taxpayers across the country. Why are you receiving this message, and what does it mean for your ITR? Have a look.

Man bleeds to death outside UP police station, had begged for help for 40 minutesAccording to his family, the victim, Rajkumar, got into an argument with an auto-rickshaw driver over the fare. As the altercation escalated, he ran towards a nearby police Pink Booth seeking help.

Caught on camera: Drunk tourists from Delhi punch, kick locals in UttarakhandUttarakhand Police arrested six drunk tourists from Rishikesh's Tapovan who were accused of beating up locals after rushing the injured to hospital.

How Sourav Joshi made his Merc. YouTuber's rags-to-crorepati story not fuelled by E20A decade ago, Sourav Joshi watched luxury cars roll into other people's driveways while helping his father at construction sites. Today, the Mercedes he drives has sparked a row after India's most popular YouTuber raised concerns over E20-blended petrol. This is what his journey was powered by. Certainly not E20.

Indian-origin astronaut Anil Menon to launch into space tonightA physician, engineer and former SpaceX flight surgeon is about to embark on his first journey beyond Earth. Here's everything you need to know.

Messi's Argentina face England next, but football can't forget the Hand of GodFIFA World Cup 2026: As England and Argentina prepare to renew one of football's fiercest rivalries, the shadow of Diego Maradona's Hand of God still looms large. Technology may have transformed the game, but some World Cup memories simply refuse to fade.

Government summons Iranian diplomats after Indian sailor killed in Hormuz strikesThe move came a day after the UAE said Iranian cruise missiles struck the tankers Mombasa and Al Bahiyah in the southern shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz, within Omani waters. The attack killed one Indian crew member and injured eight others.

sports.headline: Erling Haaland returns from the US with a raccoon, breaks the internet Despite Norway's heartbreaking World Cup exit, Erling Haaland has stolen the global spotlight by returning home with a bizarre, whiskey-clutching taxidermied raccoon. Now, the internet is completely obsessed with his viral new Texas travel companion.

1st ODI Live Score: Can Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma give India flying start? England (ENG) vs India (IND), 1st ODI Live Score: After the disappointment of the T20I series, India will be desperate to find their feet when they take on England in the first ODI at Edgbaston in Birmingham. The returns of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma, and Jasprit Bumrah have bolstered the visitors as they look to bounce back in the 50-over format.

Friction between Gambhir and Agarkar? India urged to clear confusion after UK disaster Dinesh Karthik said Gautam Gambhir and Ajit Agarkar appear out of sync after India's T20 defeats in Ireland and England. India lost T20I series in England and Ireland without even winning a game.

PV Sindhu dominates Wong Ling Ching to seal Japan Open Round 2 berth PV Sindhu made a dominant start to her Japan Open Super 750 campaign, defeating Malaysia's Wong Ling Ching 21-14, 21-11 to advance to the women's singles second round on Tuesday.

Messi's Argentina face England next, but football can't forget the Hand of God FIFA World Cup 2026: As England and Argentina prepare to renew one of football's fiercest rivalries, the shadow of Diego Maradona's Hand of God still looms large. Technology may have transformed the game, but some World Cup memories simply refuse to fade.

India honour Heather Knight, Tammy Beaumont as England legends bid farewell India paid an emotional tribute to retiring England stars Heather Knight and Tammy Beaumont after sealing a historic 270-run win in the first-ever women's Test at Lord's, with the duo receiving guard of honours and special jerseys from the visitors.

Can Afghan women dream? ICC targets 2030 qualification pathway for refuge team The ICC has set a target of integrating Afghan refugee women cricketers into qualification events by 2030, offering fresh hope to players forced into exile after the Taliban takeover in 2021.

Mbappe cleared fit as France coach plays mind games before semifinal vs Spain FIFA World Cup: Kylian Mbappe has been declared fully fit to lead France, but manager Didier Deschamps wasted no time playing mind games by labeling Spain the clear favourites ahead of their blockbuster semifinal in Dallas.

Coaching England, Brendon McCullum roots for Virat Kohli in ODI series vs India England white-ball coach Brendon McCullum lavished praise on Virat Kohli ahead of the ODI series against India, calling him one of cricket's all-time greats and hoping the former India captain enjoys a successful campaign.

Draped in diamonds, birthday boy Yamal delivers strong message against racism FIFA World Cup 2026: Sporting a stunning self-gifted diamond necklace on his 19th birthday, Spain prodigy Lamine Yamal lit up Dallas with a powerful message of integration and unity ahead of a highly anticipated clash against France.

France vs Spain: Mbappe and Yamal headline World Cup semi-final showdown FIFA World Cup 2026: France and Spain meet in the World Cup semi-final in Dallas after contrasting runs to the last four. The tie pits Mbappe's direct attacking threat against Spain's control, with a place in the final at stake.

Harry Kane blames English mentality for rumours of rift before Argentina semifinalFIFA World Cup: Harry Kane has dismissed suggestions of a rift between Thomas Tuchel and the England squad following Jude Bellingham's emotional post-match remarks after the quarter-final win over Norway, insisting the camp remains united as focus shifts to the World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen and Sat-Chi begin tune-up for World ChampionshipsPV Sindhu and Sat-Chi lead India's Japan Open campaign in Tokyo. The Super 750 offers World Championships preparation as Sat-Chi return from injury and India chase a first elusive title.

1st ODI preview: India rely on comeback men Rohit, Virat to lift dampened spiritsEngland vs India, 1st ODI preview: India will look to seal their first win on the tour after a disastrous T20I series in England. The return of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and Jasprit Bumrah adds firepower to the side, led by Shubman Gill.

Why the football world has turned against ArgentinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's rise from Messi's tragic nearly-men to world champions has changed how football fans view them. Their success now attracts suspicion, backlash and scrutiny that often follows title-winning sides.

Arshad Nadeem confirms participation in CWG 2026, set to face Neeraj ChopraThe Neeraj Chopra-Arshad Nadeem rivalry is set for another chapter after the Pakistan star confirmed his participation in the Commonwealth Games 2026. Nadeem will defend the gold medal he won in Birmingham, while Chopra returns to the Games after missing the previous edition through injury.

RCB coach Andy Flower will not leave IPL to join England Test team: KarthikRCB head coach Andy Flower is one of the early favourites to become the head coach of the England Test team. However, former India cricketer, Dinesh Karthik believes that there is one small problem which would stop the coach from switching sides to England.

Brendon McCullum breaks silence after sacking from England Test coach roleBrendon McCullum said he was disappointed after losing the England Test job and apologised to supporters. He said that Bazball changed the side's identity, but failures in the Ashes and against India proved decisive.

Harmanpreet calls for more women's Test matches after historic win at Lord'sIndia captain Harmanpreet Kaur has called for more women's Test matches after leading her side to a historic 270-run victory over England at Lord's. Speaking after India's massive win, she hoped that the team gets "ten more Test matches" to continue proving themselves in the longest format.

Loved Sooryavanshi's Wimbledon look? Decoding his Centre Court style gameLiked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's sharp look at the All England Club? From his structured black suit and striped club tie to those sleek leather loafers, we decode the teenage batting prodigy's effortless, last-minute Wimbledon style game.

Retirement far off, Kohli helping India plan for ODI World Cup 2027India captain Shubman Gill has revealed that former India captain Virat Kohli is heavily involved in the planning of ODI World Cup 2027. Gill also answered queries about Rohit Sharma and his place in the team going into the marquee tournament.

History at Home of Cricket: India win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sIt took more than 140 years for Lord's to host its first-ever women's Test. India made the wait worthwhile, hammering England by 270 runs. Yastika Bhatia's century, Smriti Mandhana's twin fifties and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul powered a landmark victory at the Home of Cricket.

Sachin drops in at Lord's, leads pep talk as India women chase historic winSachin Tendulkar paid a surprise visit to the Indian women's team at Lord's ahead of the fourth day of the historic one-off Test against England. He was seen leading the talk to the team as Harmanpreet Kaur's side closed in on a famous victory.

Day 4 Highlights: India win by 270 runs, win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sEngland (ENG) vs India (IND) Day 4 Highlights, women's one-off Test: Harmanpreet Kaur's India win the first ever women's Test match at Lord's. India beat England by a massive margin of 270 runs.

After dashing Wimbledon appearance, Sanju Samson reveals Novak Djokovic inspirationAt Wimbledon, Sanju Samson said Novak Djokovic's discipline and demeanour remain a benchmark for athletes. Yuvraj Singh backed that view, highlighting Djokovic's self-belief, longevity and influence on younger players across the globe.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup winAustralia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

How Sachin Tendulkar is backing a quiet sporting revolution in BastarSachin Tendulkar visited Dantewada to support Bastar's Maidan Cup and meet village children. The initiative is expanding community playgrounds and restoring confidence through sport in a region shaped by insurgency.

Shreyas Iyer's interview over Sooryavanshi snub irks ex-captain: Not the way to talkKris Srikkanth criticised Shreyas Iyer and the team management for dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Southampton T20I. He said the move and Iyer's remarks showed poor handling of a young player.

IPL: Stephen Fleming steps down as CSK head coach after 17 years at helmIPL: Chennai Super Kings parted ways with head coach Stephen Fleming after an 18-year association, drawing the curtains on the IPL's most successful coaching era. CSK finished three consecutive seasons outside the top four.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken?India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Andy Flower took England to No.1: Nasser backs RCB coach to replace McCullumFormer England captain Nasser Hussain has backed Andy Flower to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test head coach, saying his meticulous approach is exactly what the team needs after the Bazball era.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
